STANDARD FIRE AND SPECIAL PERIL POLICY
WORDING
Standard Fire And Special Peril Policy  Commercial (UIN) 1 | Pa ge

This page has been left blank intentionally
Standard Fire And Special Peril Policy  Commercial (UIN) 2 | Pa ge

STANDARD FIRE AND SPECIAL PERIL POLICY
PREMABLE
IN CONSIDERATION OF the Insured named in the Schedule hereto having paid to the Edelweiss
General Insurance Company Limited (hereinafter called the Company) the full premium mentioned in the
said schedule, THE COMPANY AGREES, (Subject to the Conditions and Exclusions contained herein or
endorsed or otherwise expressed hereon) that if after payment of the premium the Property insured
described in the said Schedule or any part of such Property be destroyed or damaged by any of the perils
specified hereunder during the period of insurance named in the said schedule or of any subsequent period
in respect of which the Insured shall have paid and the Company shall have accepted the premium
required for the renewal of the policy, the Company shall pay to the Insured the value of the Property at
the time of the happening of its destruction or the amount of such damage or at its option reinstate or
replace such property or any part thereof
1.

Fire:
Excluding destruction or damage caused to the property insured by
a) (i) Its own fermentation, natural heating or spontaneous combustion.
(ii) Its undergoing any heating or drying process.
b) Burning of property insured by order of any Public Authority
2.

Explosion/implosion:
Excluding loss, destruction of or damage
a) To boilers (other than domestic boilers), economisers or other vessels, machinery or apparatus
(in which steam is generated) or their contents resulting from their own explosion/implosion,
b) Caused by centrifugal forces.
4.

Aircraft Damage:
Loss, Destruction or damage caused by Aircraft, other aerial or space devices and articles
dropped there from excluding those caused by pressure waves.
5.

Riot, Strike and Malicious Damage:
Loss of or visible physical damage or destruction by external violent means directly caused to the
property insured but excluding those caused by:
a) Total or partial cessation of work or the retardation or interruption or cessation of any process
or operations or omissions of any kind.
b) Permanent or temporary dispossession resulting from confiscation, commandeering,
requisition or destruction by order of the Government or any lawfully constituted Authority.
c) Permanent or temporary dispossession of any building or plant or unit or machinery resulting
from the unlawful occupation by any person of such building or plant or unit or machinery or
prevention of access to the same.

Standard Fire And Special Peril Policy  Commercial (UIN) 3 | Pa ge

d) Burglary, housebreaking, theft, larceny or any such attempt or any omission of any kind of any
person (whether or not such act is committed in the course of a disturbance of public peace) in
any malicious act.
6.

Storm, Cyclone, Typhoon, Tempest, Hurricane, Tornado, Flood and Inundation:
Loss, destruction or damage directly caused by Storm, Cyclone, Typhoon, Tempest, Hurricane,
Tornado, Flood or Inundation excluding those resulting from earthquake, Volcanic eruption or
other convulsions of nature.
7.

Impact Damage:
Loss of or visible physical damage or destruction caused to the property insured due to impact by
any Rail/ Road vehicle or animal by direct contact not belonging to or owned by:
a) the Insured or any occupier of the premises or
b) their employees while acting in the course of their employment
8.

Subsidence and Landslide including Rock slide:
Loss, destruction or damage directly caused by Subsidence of part of the site on which the
property stands or Land slide/Rock slide excluding:
a) the normal cracking, settlement or bedding down of new structures
b) the settlement or movement of made up ground
c) coastal or river erosion
d) defective design or workmanship or use of defective materials
e) demolition, construction, structural alterations or repair of any property or groundwork or
excavations
9.

Bursting and/or overflowing of Water Tanks, Apparatus and Pipes.
10.

Missile testing operations
11.

Leakage from Automatic Sprinkler Installations,
Excluding loss, destruction or damage caused by
a) Repairs or alterations to the buildings or premises
b) Repairs, Removal or Extension of the Sprinkler Installation
c) Defects in construction known to the Insured
12.

Bush Fire
Excluding loss, destruction or damage caused by Forest Fire.

PROVIDED that the liability of the Company shall in no case exceed in respect of each item the sum
expressed in the said Schedule to be insured thereon or in the whole the total Sum Insured hereby or such
other sum or sums as may be substituted therefore by memorandum hereon or attached hereto signed by
or on behalf of the Company.
(A) GENERAL EXCLUSIONS
1.

This Policy does not cover (not applicable to policies covering dwellings) the Excess applicable,
as mentioned in the policy schedule.
The Excess shall apply per event per insured.

Standard Fire And Special Peril Policy  Commercial (UIN) 4 | Pa ge

2.

Loss, destruction, or damage caused by war, invasion, act of foreign enemy hostilities or war like
operations (whether war be declared or not), civil war, mutiny, civil commotion assuming the
proportions of or amounting to a popular rising, military rising, rebellion, revolution, insurrection
or military or usurped power
3.

Loss, destruction, or damage directly or indirectly caused to the property insured by
a) ionizing radiations or contamination by radioactivity from any nuclear fuel or
b) from any nuclear waste from the combustion of nuclear fuel
c) the radio active toxic, explosive or other hazardous properties of any explosive nuclear
assembly or nuclear component thereof
4.

Loss, destruction, or damage caused to the insured property by pollution or contamination
excluding
a) pollution or contamination which itself results from a peril hereby insured against.
b) any peril hereby insured against which itself results from pollution or contamination
5.

Loss, destruction, or damage to bullion or unset precious stones, any curios or works of art for an
amount exceeding Rs.10000/-, goods held in trust or on commission, manuscripts, plans,
drawings, securities, obligations or documents of any kind, stamps, coins, or paper money,
cheques, books of accounts or other business books, computer systems records, explosives unless
otherwise expressly stated in the policy.
6.

Loss, destruction, or damage to the stocks in Cold Storage premises caused by change of
temperature.
7.

Loss, destruction or damage to any electrical machine, apparatus, fixture or fitting and arising
from or occasioned by over-running, excessive pressure, short circuiting, arcing, self heating or
leakage of electricity from whatever cause (lightning included) provided that this exclusion shall
apply only to the particular machine, apparatus, fixture or fitting so affected and not to other
machines, apparatus, fixtures or fittings which may be destroyed or damaged by fire so set up.
8.

Expenses necessarily incurred on (i) Architects, Surveyors, and Consulting Engineer's Fees and
(ii) Debris Removal by the Insured following a loss, destruction or damage to the Property
insured by an insured peril in excess of 3% and 1% of the claim amount respectively
9.

Loss of earnings, loss by delay, loss of market or other consequential or indirect loss or damage
of any kind or description whatsoever.
10.

Loss, or damage by spoilage resulting from the retardation or interruption or cessation of any
process or operation caused by operation of any of the perils covered.
11.

Loss by theft during or after the occurrence of any insured peril except as provided under Riot,
Strike and, Malicious damage
Standard Fire And Special Peril Policy  Commercial (UIN) 5 | Pa ge

12.

Any Loss or damage occasioned by or through or in consequence directly or indirectly due to
earthquake, volcanic eruption, or other convulsions of nature.
13.

Loss or damage to property insured if removed to any building or place other than in which it is
herein stated to be insured, except machinery and equipment temporarily removed for repairs,
cleaning, renovation, or other similar purposes for a period not exceeding 60 days
14.

Terrorism Damage: This Policy excludes loss, damage, cost, or expense of whatsoever nature
directly or indirectly caused by, resulting from or in connection with any act of terrorism
regardless of any other cause or event contributing concurrently or in any other sequence to the
loss.

This exclusion also includes loss, damage, cost, or expense of whatsoever nature directly or
indirectly caused by, resulting from or in connection with any action taken in controlling,
preventing, suppressing or in any way relating to the above
(B) GENERAL CONDITIONS
1.

This Policy shall be voidable in the event of mis-representation, mis-description or non-disclosure
of any material particular.
2.

All insurances under this policy shall cease on expiry of seven days from the date of fall or
displacement of any building or part thereof or of the whole or any part of any range of buildings
or of any structure of which such building forms part.

PROVIDED such a fall or displacement is not caused by insured perils, loss or damage which is
covered by this policy or would be covered if such building, range of buildings or structure were
insured under this policy.

Notwithstanding the above, the Company subject to an express notice being given as soon as
possible but not later than seven days of any such fall or displacement may agree to continue the
insurance subject to revised rates, terms and conditions as may be decided by it and confirmed in
writing to this effect.
3.

Under any of the following circumstances the insurance ceases to attach as regards the property
affected unless the Insured, before the occurrence of any loss or damage, obtains the sanction of
the Company signified by endorsement upon the policy by or on behalf of the Company:
a) If the trade or manufacture carried on be altered, or if the nature of the occupation of or other
circumstances affecting the building insured or containing the insured property be changed in
such a way as to increase the risk of loss or damage by Insured Perils.
b) If the building insured or containing the insured property becomes unoccupied and so remains
for a period of more than 30 days (to be deleted in case of dwellings)
c) If the interest in the property passes from the insured otherwise than by will or operation of
law.

Standard Fire And Special Peril Policy  Commercial (UIN) 6 | Pa ge

4.

This insurance does not cover any loss or damage to property which, at the time of the happening
of such loss or damage, is insured by or would, but for the existence of this policy, be insured by
any marine policy or policies except in respect of any excess beyond the amount which would
have been payable under the marine policy or policies had this insurance not been effected
5.

This Insurance may be terminated at any time at the request of the Insured, in which case the
company will retain the premium at customary short period rate for the time policy has been in
force.

This insurance may also at any time be terminated at the option of the Company, on 15 days'
notice to that effect being given to the Insured, in which case the Company shall be liable to
repay on demand a rateable proportion of the premium for the unexpired term from the date of the
cancellation.

Short Period Scale
For a period not exceeding 15 days 10% of the Annual rate
-do- 1 month 15% of the Annual rate
-do- 2 months 30% of the Annual rate
-do- 3 months 40% of the Annual rate
-do- 4 months 50% of the Annual rate
-do- 5 months 60% of the Annual rate
-do- 6 months 70% of the Annual rate
-do- 7 months 75% of the Annual rate
-do- 8 months 80% of the Annual rate
-do- 9 months 85% of the Annual rate
For a period exceeding 9 months The full Annual rate
6. (I) On the happening of any loss or damage the Insured shall forthwith give notice thereof to the
Company and shall within 15 days after the loss or damage, or such further time as the Company may in
writing allow in that behalf, deliver to the Company
a) A claim in writing for the loss or damage containing as particular an account as may be reasonably
practicable of all the several articles or items or property damaged or destroyed, and of the amount of the
loss or damage thereto respectively, having regard to their value at the time of the loss or damage not
including profit of any kind.
b) Particulars of all other insurances, if any
The Insured shall also at all times at his own expense produce, procure and give to the Company all such
further particulars, plans, specification books,vouchers, invoices, duplicates or copies thereof, documents,
investigation reports (internal/external), proofs and information with respect to the claim and the origin
and cause of the loss and the circumstances under which the loss or damage occurred, and any matter
touching the liability or the amount of the liability of the Company as may be reasonably required by or
on behalf of the Company together with a declaration on oath or in other legal form of the truth of the
claim and of any matters connected therewith.

Standard Fire And Special Peril Policy  Commercial (UIN) 7 | Pa ge

No claim under this policy shall be payable unless the terms of this condition have been complied with
(ii) In no case whatsoever shall the Company be liable for any loss or damage after the expiry of 12
months from the happening of the loss or damage unless the claim is the subject of pending action or
arbitration; it being expressly agreed and declared that if the Company shall disclaim liability for any
claim hereunder and such claim shall not within 12 calendar months from the date of the disclaimer have
been made the subject matter of a suit in a court of law then the claim shall for all purposes be deemed to
have been abandoned and shall not thereafter be recoverable hereunder
7.

On the happening of loss or damage to any of the property insured by this policy, the Company may
a) Enter and take and keep possession of the building or premises where the loss or damage has
happened.
b) Take possession of or require to be delivered to it any property of the Insured in the building or
on the premises at the time of the loss or damage.
c) Keep possession of any such property and examine, sort, arrange, remove or otherwise deal
with the same.
d) Sell any such property or dispose of the same for account of whom it may Concern.

The powers conferred by this condition shall be exercisable by the Company at any time until notice in
writing is given by the insured that he makes no claim under the policy, or if any claim is made, until such
claim is finally determined or withdrawn, and the Company shall not by any act done in the exercise or
purported exercise of its powers hereunder, incur any liability to the Insured or diminish its rights to rely
upon any of the conditions of this policy in answer to any claim.
8.

If the insured or any person on his behalf shall not comply with the requirements of the Company or
shall hinder or obstruct the Company, in the exercise of its powers hereunder, all benefits under this
policy shall be forfeited.

The Insured shall not in any case be entitled to abandon any property to the
Company whether taken possession of by the Company or not.
9.

If the Company at its option, reinstate or replace the property damaged or destroyed, or any part
thereof, instead of paying the amount of the loss or damage, or join with any other Company or Insurer(s)
in so doing, the Company shall not be bound to reinstate exactly or completely but only as circumstances
permit and in reasonably sufficient manner, and in no case shall the Company be bound to expend more
in reinstatement than it would have cost to reinstate such property as it was at the time of the occurrence
of such loss or damage nor more than the sum insured by the Company thereon.

If the Company so elect
to reinstate or replace any property the insured shall at his own expense furnish the Company with such
plans, specifications, measurements, quantities and such other particulars as the Company may require,
and no acts done, or caused to be done, by the Company with a view to reinstatement or replacement shall
be deemed an election by the Company to reinstate or replace.

If in any case the Company shall be unable to reinstate or repair the property hereby insured, because of
any municipal or other regulations in force affecting the alignment of streets or the construction of
buildings or otherwise, the Company shall, in every such case, only be liable to pay such sum as would be
Standard Fire And Special Peril Policy  Commercial (UIN) 8 | Pa ge

requisite to reinstate or repair such property if the same could lawfully be reinstated to its former
condition.
10.If the property hereby insured shall at the breaking out of any fire or at the commencement of any
destruction of or damage to the property by any other peril hereby insured against be collectively of
greater value than the sum insured thereon, then the Insured shall be considered as being his own insurer
for the difference and shall bear a rateable proportion of the loss accordingly.

Every item, if more than
one, of the policy shall be separately subject to this condition.
11.If at the time of any loss or damage happening to any property hereby insured there be any other
subsisting insurance or Insurances, whether effected by the Insured or by any other person or persons
covering the same property, this Company shall not be liable to pay or contribute more than its rateable
proportion of such loss or damage.
12.The Insured shall at the expense of the Company do and concur in doing, and permit to be done, all
such acts and things as may be necessary or reasonably required by the Company for the purpose of
enforcing any rights and remedies or of obtaining relief or indemnity from other parties to which the
Company shall be or would become entitled or subrogated, upon its paying for or making good any loss
or damage under this policy, whether such acts and things shall be or become necessary or required before
or after his indemnification by the Company.
13.

If any dispute or difference shall arise as to the quantum to be paid under this policy (liability being
otherwise admitted) such difference shall independently of all other questions be referred to the decision
of a sole arbitrator to be appointed in writing by the parties to or if they cannot agree upon a single
arbitrator within 30 days of any party invoking arbitration, the same shall be referred to a panel of three
arbitrators, comprising of two arbitrators, one to be appointed by each of the parties to the
dispute/difference and the third arbitrator to be appointed by such two arbitrators and arbitration shall be
conducted under and in accordance with the provisions of the Arbitration and Conciliation Act, 1996.

It is clearly agreed and understood that no difference or dispute shall be referable to arbitration as
hereinbefore provided, if the Company has disputed or not accepted liability under or in respect of this
policy.

It is hereby expressly stipulated and declared that it shall be a condition precedent to any right of action or
suit upon this policy that the award by such arbitrator/arbitrators of the amount of the loss or damage shall
be first obtained.
14.

Every notice and other communication to the Company required by these conditions must be written
or printed.
15.At all times during the period of insurance of this policy the insurance cover will be maintained to the
full extent of the respective sum insured in consideration of which upon the settlement of any loss under
this policy, pro-rata premium for the unexpired period from the date of such loss to the expiry of period of
insurance for the amount of such loss shall be payable by the insured to the Company.

The additional
premium referred above shall be deducted from the net claim amount payable under the policy.

This
Standard Fire And Special Peril Policy  Commercial (UIN) 9 | Pa ge

continuous cover to the full extent will be available notwithstanding any previous loss for which the
company may have paid hereunder and irrespective of the fact whether the additional premium as
mentioned above has been actually paid or not following such loss.

The intention of this condition is to
ensure continuity of the cover to the insured subject only to the right of the company for deduction from
the claim amount, when settled, of pro-rata premium to be calculated from the date of loss till expiry of
the policy.

Notwithstanding what is stated above, the Sum Insured shall stand reduced by the amount of loss in case
the insured immediately on occurrence of the loss exercises his option not to reinstate the sum insured as
above.
Standard Fire And Special Peril Policy  Commercial (UIN) 10 | Pa ge

22 policy, be insured by any marine policy or policies except in respect of any excess beyond
23 the amount which would have been payable under the marine policy or policies had this
24 insurance not been effected.
25
26 CANCELLATION
27 5) This insurance may be terminated at any time at the request of the Insured, in which case
28 the Company will retain the premium at customary short period rate for the time the policy
29 has been in force.
30 This insurance may also at any time be terminated at the option of the Company, on 15
31 days' notice to that effect being given to the Insured, in which case the Company shall be
32 liable to repay on demand a rateable proportion of the premium for the unexpired term
33 from the date of the cancellation. It is however, further stated that such cancellation by the
34 insurer should only be exercised on ground of mis-representation, fraud, non-disclosure of
35 material facts and non-co-operation by the insured.
36
37 LOSS NOTIFICATION
38 6 (i) On the happening of any loss or damage the Insured shall forthwith give notice thereof to
39 the Company and shall within 15 days after the loss or damage, or such further time as the
40 Company may in writing allow in that behalf, deliver to the Company
41
42 a) A claim in writing for the loss or damage containing as particular an account as may
43 be reasonably practicable of all the several articles or items or property damaged or
4 | P a ge

1 destroyed, and of the amount of the loss or damage thereto respectively, having regard
2 to their value at the time of the loss or damage not including profit of any kind.
3
4 b) Particulars of all other insurances, if any
5
6 The Insured shall also at all times at his own expense produce, procure and give to the
7 Company all such further particulars, plans, specification books, vouchers, invoices, duplicates
8 or copies thereof, documents, investigation reports (internal/external), proofs and information
9 with respect to the claim and the origin and cause of the loss and the circumstances under
10 which the loss or damage occurred, and any matter touching the liability or the amount of the
11 liability of the Company as may be reasonably required by or on behalf of the Company
12 together with a declaration on oath or in other legal form of the truth of the claim and of any
13 matters connected therewith.
14
15 No claim under this policy shall be payable unless the terms of this condition have been
16 complied with.
17
18 6 (ii) In no case whatsoever shall the Company be liable for any loss or damage after the
19 expiration of 12 months from the happening of the loss or damage unless the claim is the
20 subject of pending action or arbitration; it being expressly agreed and declared that if the
21 Company shall disclaim liability for any claim hereunder and such claim shall not within 12
22 calendar months from the date of the disclaimer have been made the subject matter of a suit in
23 a court of law then the claim shall for all purposes be deemed to have been abandoned and
24 shall not thereafter be recoverable hereunder.
25
26 RIGHT OF ENTRY
27 7) On the happening of loss or damage to any of the property insured by this policy, the
28 Company may
29
30 a) enter and take and keep possession of the building or premises where the loss or
31 damage has happened.
32 b) take possession of or require to be delivered to it any property of the Insured in the
33 building or on the premises at the time of the loss or damage.
34 c) keep possession of any such property and examine, sort, arrange, remove, or otherwise
35 deal with the same.
36 d) sell any such property or dispose of the same for account of whom it may Concern.
37
38 The powers conferred by this condition shall be exercisable by the Company at any time until
39 notice in writing is given by the insured that he makes no claim under the policy, or if any claim
40 is made, until such claim is finally determined or withdrawn, and the Company shall not by any
41 act done in the exercise or purported exercise of its powers hereunder, incur any liability to the
42 Insured or diminish its rights to rely upon any of the conditions of this policy in answer to any
43 claim.
44
5 | P a ge

1 If the insured or any person on his behalf shall not comply with the requirements of the
2 Company or shall hinder or obstruct the Company, in the exercise of its powers hereunder, all
3 benefits under this policy shall be forfeited.
4
5 The Insured shall not in any case be entitled to abandon any property to the Company whether
6 taken possession of by the Company or not.
7
8 FORFEITURE
9 8) If the claim be in any respect fraudulent, or if any false declaration be made or used in
10 support thereof or if any fraudulent means or devices are used by the Insured or any one
11 acting on his behalf to obtain any benefit under the policy or if the loss or damage be
12 occasioned by the willful act, or with the connivance of the Insured, all benefits under this
13 policy shall be forfeited.
14
15 OPTIONS TO REINSTATE
16 9) If the Company at its option, reinstate or replace the property damaged or destroyed, or
17 any part thereof, instead of paying the amount of the loss or damage, or join with any other
18 Company or Insurer(s) in so doing, the Company shall not be bound to reinstate exactly or
19 completely but only as circumstances permit and in reasonably sufficient manner, and in no
20 case shall the Company be bound to expend more in reinstatement than it would have cost
21 to reinstate such property as it was at the time of the occurrence of such loss or damage
22 nor more than the sum insured by the Company thereon. If the Company so elect to
23 reinstate or replace any property the insured shall at his own expense furnish the Company
24 with such plans, specifications, measurements, quantities, and such other particulars as the
25 Company may require, and no acts done, or caused to be done, by the Company with a view
26 to reinstate or replace shall be deemed an election by the Company to reinstate or replace.
27
28 If in any case the Company shall be unable to reinstate or repair the property hereby
29 insured, because of any municipal or other regulations in force affecting the alignment of
30 streets or the construction of buildings or otherwise, the Company shall, in every such case,
31 only be liable to pay such sum as would be requisite to reinstate or repair such property if
32 the same could lawfully be reinstated to its former condition.
33
34 CONDITION OF AVERAGE
35 10) If the property hereby insured shall at the breaking out of any fire or at the commencement
36 of any destruction of or damage to the property by any other peril hereby insured against
37 be collectively of greater value than the sum insured thereon, then the Insured shall be
38 considered as being his own insurer for the difference and shall bear a rateable proportion
39 of the loss accordingly. Every item, if more than one, of the policy shall be separately
40 subject to this condition.
41
42 CONTRIBUTION
43 11) If at the time of any loss or damage happening to any property hereby insured there be any
44 other subsisting insurance or insurances, whether effected by the Insured or by any other
6 | P a ge

1 person or persons covering the same property, this Company shall not be liable to pay or
2 contribute more than its rateable proportion of such loss or damage.
3
4 SUBROGATION
5 12) The Insured shall at the expense of the Company do and concur in doing, and permit to be
6 done, all such acts and things as may be necessary or reasonably required by the Company
7 for the purpose of enforcing any rights and remedies or of obtaining relief or indemnity
8 from other parties to which the Company shall be or would become entitled or subrogated,
9 upon its paying for or making good any loss or damage under this policy, whether such acts
10 and things shall be or become necessary or required before or after his indemnification by
11 the Company.
12
13 ARBITRATION
14 13) If any dispute or difference shall arise as to the quantum to be paid under this policy
15 (liability being otherwise admitted) such difference shall independently of all other
16 questions be referred to the decision of a sole arbitrator to be appointed in writing by the
17 parties to or if they cannot agree upon a single arbitrator within 30 days of any party
18 invoking arbitration, the same shall be referred to a panel of three arbitrators, comprising of
19 two arbitrators, one to be appointed by each of the parties to the dispute/difference and
20 the third arbitrator to be appointed by such two arbitrators and arbitration shall be
21 conducted under and in accordance with the provisions of the Arbitration and Conciliation
22 Act, 1996.
23
24 It is clearly agreed and understood that no difference or dispute shall be referable to arbitration
25 as hereinbefore provided, if the Company has disputed or not accepted liability under or in
26 respect of this policy.
27
28 It is hereby expressly stipulated and declared that it shall be a condition precedent to any right
29 of action or suit upon this policy that the award by such arbitrator/ arbitrators of the amount of
30 the loss or damage shall be first obtained.
31
32 NOTICE
33 14) Every notice and other communication to the Company required by these conditions must
34 be written or printed.
35
36 REINSTATEMENT OF SUM INSURED
37 15) At all times during the period of insurance of this policy the insurance cover will be
38 maintained to the full extent of the respective sum insured in consideration of which upon
39 the settlement of any loss under this policy, pro-rata premium for the unexpired period
40 from the date of such loss to the expiry of period of insurance for the amount of such loss
41 shall be payable by the insured to the Company.
42
43 The additional premium referred above shall be deducted from the net claim amount
44 payable under the policy. This continuous cover to the full extent will be available
7 | P a ge

1 notwithstanding any previous loss for which the company may have paid hereunder and
2 irrespective of the fact whether the additional premium as mentioned above has been
3 actually paid or not following such loss. The intention of this condition is to ensure
4 continuity of the cover to the insured subject only to the right of the company for deduction
5 from the claim amount, when settled, of pro-rata premium to be calculated from the date
6 of loss till expiry of the policy.
7
8 Notwithstanding what is stated above, the Sum Insured shall stand reduced by the amount of
9 loss in case the insured immediately on occurrence of the loss exercises his option not to
10 reinstate the sum insured as above.
11
12
13 E. SPECIAL CONDITIONS:
14
15 a) Basis of Sum insured:
16
17 (i) Market Value Basis
18 It is a requirement of this Insurance that the sums insured stated in the Schedule, as declared
19 by the insured shall not be less than the  Market value  of the property insured.
20
21 (ii) Reinstatement Value Basis
22 The insured will also have an option to insure the property (except for stocks) on a
23  Reinstatement value basis , which shall be the cost of replacing or reinstating on the same site
24 or any other site with property of the same kind or type but not superior to or more extensive
25 then the insured property when new as on date of the loss, subject to special provisions
26 contained elsewhere in this Policy.
27
28 b) Basis of Settlement:
29
30 (i) Market Value settlement
31 It is the actual cost of the property lost or damaged at the time of loss (excluding profit of any
32 kind) taking into consideration the wear and tear and depreciation for usage.
33
34 (ii) Reinstatement Value settlement
35
36 The following are the provisions of Reinstatement Value Settlement
37
38 1. The work of replacement or reinstatement (which may be carried out upon another site
39 and in any manner suitable to the requirements of the insured subject to the liability of
40 the Company not being thereby increased) must be commenced and carried out with
41 reasonable dispatch and in any case must be completed within 12 months after the
42 destruction or damage or within such further time as the Company may in writing allow,
43 otherwise no payment beyond the amount which would have been payable under the
44 policy if this memorandum had not been incorporated therein shall be made.
8 | P a ge

1
2 2. Until expenditure has been incurred by the Insured in replacing or reinstating the
3 property destroyed or damaged the Company shall not be liable for any payment in
4 excess of the amount which would have been payable under the policy if this
5 memorandum had not been incorporated therein.
6
7 3. If at the time of replacement or reinstatement the sum representing the cost which
8 would have been incurred in replacement or reinstatement if the whole of the property
9 covered had been destroyed, exceeds the Sum Insured thereon or at the
10 commencement of any destruction or damage to such property by any of the perils
11 insured against by the policy, then the insured shall be considered as being his own
12 insurer for the excess and shall bear a rateable proportion of the loss accordingly. Each
13 item of the policy (if more than one) to which this memorandum applies shall be
14 separately subject to the foregoing provision.
15
16 4. This Memorandum shall be without force or effect if
17
18 a) the Insured fails to intimate to the Company within 6 months from the date of
19 destruction or damage or such further time as the Company may in writing allow his
20 intention to replace or reinstate the property destroyed or damaged.
21
22 b) the Insured is unable or unwilling to replace or reinstate the property destroyed or
23 damaged on the same or another site.
24
25 Expenses Covered
26
27 (i) Architects, Surveyors and consulting engineer s fees (upto 3% of the claim amount)
28
29  It is hereby declared and understood that the expenses incurred towards Architects, Surveyors
30 and Consulting Engineers fees for plans, specification tenders, quantities and services in
31 connection with the superintendence of the reinstatement for the Building, Machinery,
32 Accessories and equipment insured under this policy is covered upto 3% of the adjusted loss,
33 but it is understood that this does not include any costs in connection with the preparation of
34 the Insured s claim or estimate of loss in the event of damage by insured perils .
35
36 (ii) Removal of debris clause (upto 1% of the claim amount)
37
38  It is hereby declared and agreed that the expenses incurred upto 1% of the claim amount is
39 included in the sum insured thereon for
40
41 (a) Removal of debris from the premises of the Insured;
42 (b) dismantling or demolishing;
43 (c) shoring up or propping. 
44
9 | P a ge

1 Note: (b) & (c) above should be deleted when neither Building nor Machinery are covered.
2
3 F. DEFINITIONS:
4
5 1) Fire: Fire means combustion which is accidental accompanied by visible flame or glow
6 excluding destruction or damage caused to the property insured by (i) its own fermentation,
7 natural heating, or spontaneous combustion, (ii) its undergoing any heating or drying
8 process and (iii) burning of property insured by order of any Public Authority, -including
9 damage by fire initiated from such causes to the said property. However, ensuing damage
10 by spread of fire to other property insured is payable provided such damage is not caused
11 by peril otherwise excluded under the policy.
12
13 2) Lightning: Loss or Damage Directly caused by Lightning
14
15 3) Explosion/Implosion: Damage excluding loss, destruction of or damage (a) to boilers (other
16 than domestic boilers), economizers or other vessels, machinery, or apparatus (in which
17 steam is generated) or their contents resulting from their own explosion/implosion, (b)
18 caused by centrifugal forces.
19
20 4) Aircraft Damage: Loss, Destruction or damage caused by Aircraft, other aerial or space
21 devices and articles dropped therefrom excluding those caused by pressure waves.
22
23 5) Riot, Strike, and Malicious Damage: Loss of or visible physical damage or destruction by
24 external violent means directly caused to the property insured but excluding those caused
25 by
26 i. total or partial cessation of work or the retardation or interruption or cessation of any
27 process or operations or omissions of any kind.
28 ii. Permanent or temporary dispossession resulting from confiscation, commandeering,
29 requisition, or destruction by order of the Government or any lawfully constituted Authority.
30 iii. Permanent or temporary dispossession of any building or plant or unit or machinery
31 resulting from the unlawful occupation by any person of such building or plant or unit or
32 machinery or prevention of access to the same.
33 iv. Burglary, housebreaking, theft, looting, larceny or any such attempt or any omission of
34 any kind of any person (whether or not such act is committed in the course of a
35 disturbance of public peace) in any malicious act.
36
37 6) Storm, Cyclone, Typhoon, Tempest, Hurricane, Tornado, Flood, and Inundation: Loss,
38 destruction or damage directly caused by Storm, Cyclone, Typhoon, Tempest, Hurricane,
39 Tornado, Flood, or Inundation excluding those resulting from earthquake, Volcanic
40 eruption, or other convulsions of nature. (Wherever earthquake cover is given as an  add
41 on cover  the words  excluding those resulting from earthquake, volcanic eruption or other
42 convulsions of nature  shall stand deleted.)
43
44
10 | P a ge

1
2 7. Bursting and/ or overflowing of water tanks, apparatus, and pipes:
3 Bursting or breaking of water tanks, pipes, or apparatus due to the buildup of internal
4 pressure beyond their capacity or breaking point and Overflowing is the escape of water
5 from the top of an overfilled container, water tanks, apparatus, or pipes
6
7 8. Impact Damage:
8 Loss of or visible physical damage or destruction caused to the property insured due to
9 impact by any Rail/ Road vehicle or animal by direct contact not belonging to or owned
10 by (a) the Insured or any occupier of the premises or (b) their employees while acting in
11 the course of their employment.
12
13 9. Subsidence and Landslide including Rock slide:
14 a) Loss, destruction, or damage directly caused by Subsidence of part of the site on
15 which the property stands or Land slide/Rock slide excluding:
16 b) the normal cracking, settlement or bedding down of new structures
17 c) the settlement or movement of made up ground
18 d) coastal or river erosion
19 e) defective design or workmanship or use of defective materials
20 f) demolition, construction, structural alterations or repair of any property or
21 groundworks or excavations.
22 10) Leakage from Automatic Sprinkler Installations: Excluding loss, destruction, or damage
23 caused by
24 a) Repairs or alterations to the buildings or premises
25 b) Repairs, Removal, or Extension of the Sprinkler Installation
26 c) Defects in construction known to the Insured.
27
28 11) Bush Fire: Loss excluding, destruction or damage caused by Forest Fire.
29
30 12) Terrorism:
31 An act of terrorism means an act or series of acts, including but not limited to the use of
32 force or violence and/or the threat thereof, of any person or group(s) of persons, whether
33 acting alone or on behalf of or in connection with any organisation(s) or government(s), or
34 unlawful associations, recognized under Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Amendment Act,
35 2008 or any other related and applicable national or state legislation formulated to combat
36 unlawful and terrorist activities in the nation for the time being in force, committed for
37 political, religious, ideological or similar purposes including the intention to influence any
38 government and/or to put the public or any section of the public in fear for such purposes.
39
40 13) Excess means the amount stated in the Schedule, which shall be borne by the Insured in
41 respect of each and every Claim made under this Policy.
42
11 | P a ge

1 14) Reinstatement Value: It is cost which would be incurred to replace the property insured at
2 the Premises with new items of similar kind and quality at any time during the Period of
3 Insurance.
4
5
6 15)  Market Value  means replacement value of insured property or item as New at the time of
7 Damage or Loss less due allowance for betterment, wear, and tear and/or depreciation on
8 the date of loss excluding the cost of land.
9
10 16)  Property Damage  means actual sudden and accidental physical damage to the Insured
11 Premises or property insured under the policy caused by the perils covered.
12
13
14 17)  Property  means assets, machinery, equipment, furniture, fixtures and fittings, electrical
15 installations and stock and stock-in-trade in the Insured s premises described in the
16 Schedule to this Policy including items contained therein for which the Insured is
17 accountable.
18
19 18)  Period of Insurance  means the period during which cover is provided by this Policy of
20 Insurance as specified in The Schedule.
21
22
23 19)  Proposal  means any signed proposal by filling up the questionnaires and declarations,
24 written statements and any information in addition thereto supplied to Company by Insured
25 or on Insured s behalf.
26
27 20)  Policy  means the Policy Booklet, the Schedule, the Proposal and any applicable
28 endorsements or memoranda. The policy contains the details of the extent of the cover
29 available to the Insured, what is excluded from the cover and the conditions, warranties on
30 which the Policy is issued.
31
32
33 21)  Schedule  means the latest schedule issued by the Company as part of the Policy. It
34 provides details of the Insured s Policy including full description of properties covered which
35 are in force and the period of cover against the properties described.
36
37 22)  Sum Insured  means the amount of insurance cover specified in The Schedule in respect of
38 any particular item of Property Insured or interest insured or the total amount of insurance
39 cover specified in The Schedule as the context may require.
12 | P a ge

1 23)  Occupancy  means the activity carried out in the insured premised stated in the schedule
2 and / or the purpose for which the same is used.
3
4 24)  Claim  means a claim under an Operative Clause in respect of an insured event.
5
13 | P a ge

pages.text:
1 GENERAL INSURANCE COUNCIL
2
3 ANNEXURE III
4
5 INDIA MARKET WORDING
6 STANDARD FIRE AND SPECIAL PERILS POLICY
7 (MATERIAL DAMAGE)   (COMMERCIAL RISKS)
8 CHANGES HIGHLIGHTED IN RED COLOUR
9
10 IN CONSIDERATION OF the Insured named in the Schedule hereto having paid to the  ..
11 General Insurance Company Limited (hereinafter called the Company) the full premium
12 mentioned in the said schedule, THE COMPANY AGREES, (Subject to the Conditions and
13 Exclusions contained herein or endorsed or otherwise expressed hereon) that if after payment
14 of the premium the Property insured described in the said Schedule or any part of such
15 Property be accidentally and physically , destroyed or damaged by any of the perils specified in
16 paragraph (A) hereunder during the period of insurance named in the said schedule or of any
17 subsequent period in respect of which the Insured shall have paid and the Company shall have
18 accepted the premium required for the renewal of the policy, the Company shall pay to the
19 Insured the value of the Property at the time of the happening of its destruction or the amount
20 of such damage or at its option reinstate or replace such property or any part thereof:
21
22 A. PERILS COVERED
23
24 1. Fire
25 2. Lightning
26 3. Explosion/Implosion
27 4. Aircraft Damage
28 5. Riot, Strike and Malicious Damage
29 6. Storm, Cyclone, Typhoon, Tempest, Hurricane, Tornado, Flood and Inundation
30 7. Impact Damage
31 8. Subsidence and Landslide including Rock slide
32 9. Bursting and/or overflowing of Water Tanks, Apparatus and Pipes
33 10. Missile testing operations
34 11. Leakage from Automatic Sprinkler Installations
35 12. Bush Fire
36
37 B. LIMIT OF INDEMNITY
38
39 PROVIDED that the liability of the Company shall in no case exceed in respect of each item the
40 sum expressed in the said Schedule to be insured thereon or in the whole the total Sum Insured
41 hereby or such other sum or sums as may be substituted therefor by memorandum hereon or
42 attached hereto signed by or on behalf of the Company.
43
44
1 | P a ge

1 C. GENERAL EXCLUSIONS
2
3 1) This Policy does not cover (not applicable to policies covering dwellings) the Excess
4 applicable, as mentioned in the policy schedule. The Excess shall apply per event per
5 insured.
6
7 2) Loss, destruction, or damage caused by war, invasion, act of foreign enemy hostilities or war
8 like operations (whether war be declared or not), civil war, mutiny, civil commotion
9 assuming the proportions of or amounting to a popular rising, military rising, rebellion,
10 revolution, insurrection or military or usurped power.
11
12 3) Loss, destruction, or damage directly or indirectly caused to the property insured by
13
14 a) ionizing radiations or contamination by radioactivity from any nuclear fuel or
15 b) from any nuclear waste from the combustion of nuclear fuel
16 c) the radio active toxic, explosive or other hazardous properties of any explosive nuclear
17 assembly or nuclear component thereof
18
19 4) Loss, destruction, or damage caused to the insured property by pollution or contamination
20 excluding
21
22 a) pollution or contamination which itself results from a peril hereby insured against.
23 b) any peril hereby insured against which itself results from pollution or contamination
24
25 5) Loss, destruction, or damage to bullion or unset precious stones, any curios or works of art
26 for an amount exceeding Rs.10000/-, goods held in trust or on commission, manuscripts,
27 plans, drawings, securities, obligations or documents of any kind, stamps, coins, or paper
28 money, cheques, books of accounts or other business books, computer systems records,
29 explosives unless otherwise expressly stated in the policy.
30
31 6) Loss, destruction, or damage to the stocks in Cold Storage premises caused by change of
32 temperature.
33
34 7) Loss, destruction or damage to any electrical machine, electronic equipment, apparatus,
35 fixture or fitting and arising from or occasioned by over-running, excessive pressure, short
36 circuiting, arcing, self heating or leakage of electricity from whatever cause (lightning
37 included) provided that this exclusion shall apply only to the particular machine, equipment,
38 apparatus, fixture or fitting so affected and not to other machines, equipment, apparatus,
39 fixtures or fittings which may be destroyed or damaged by fire so set up.
40
41 8) Expenses necessarily incurred on (i) Architects, Surveyors, and Consulting Engineer's Fees
42 and (ii) Debris Removal by the Insured following a loss, destruction or damage to the
43 Property insured by an insured peril in excess of 3% and 1% of the claim amount
44 respectively as mentioned under paragraph (E) Special Conditions.
2 | P a ge

1
2 9) Loss of earnings, loss by delay, loss of market or other consequential or indirect loss or
3 damage of any kind or description whatsoever.
4
5 10) Loss, or damage by spoilage resulting from the retardation or interruption or cessation of
6 any process or operation caused by operation of any of the perils covered.
7
8 11) Loss by theft during or after the occurrence of any insured peril except as provided under
9 Riot, Strike and, Malicious damage.
10
11 12) Any Loss or damage occasioned by or through or in consequence directly or indirectly due
12 to earthquake, volcanic eruption, or other convulsions of nature.
13
14 13) Loss or damage to property insured if removed to any building or place other than in which
15 it is herein stated to be insured, except machinery and equipment temporarily removed for
16 repairs, cleaning, renovation, or other similar purposes for a period not exceeding 60 days.
17
18 14) Terrorism Damage: This Policy excludes loss, damage, cost, or expense of whatsoever
19 nature directly or indirectly caused by, resulting from or in connection with any act of
20 terrorism regardless of any other cause or event contributing concurrently or in any other
21 sequence to the loss.
22
23 This exclusion also includes loss, damage, cost, or expense of whatsoever nature directly or
24 indirectly caused by, resulting from or in connection with any action taken in controlling,
25 preventing, suppressing or in any way relating to the above.
26
27 15) Loss, destruction or damage to Land, Livestock, Blood stock, Agricultural risks,
28 Growing crops, or Trees
29
30 D. GENERAL CONDITIONS
31
32 MISREPRESENATION
33 1) THIS POLICY shall be voidable in the event of mis-representation, mis-description, or non-
34 disclosure of any material particular.
35
36 FALL OR DISPLACEMENT OF BUILDINGS
37 2) All insurances under this policy shall cease on expiry of seven days from the date of fall or
38 displacement of any building or part thereof or of the whole or any part of any range of
39 buildings or of any structure of which such building forms part.
40
41 PROVIDED such a fall or displacement is not caused by insured perils, loss or damage which is
42 covered by this policy or would be covered if such building, range of buildings or structure were
43 insured under this policy.
44
3 | P a ge

1 Notwithstanding the above, the Company subject to an express notice being given as soon as
2 possible but not later than seven days of any such fall or displacement may agree to continue
3 the insurance subject to revised rates, terms and conditions as may be decided by it and
4 confirmed in writing to this effect.
5
6 ALTERATION
7 3) Under any of the following circumstances the insurance ceases to attach as regards the
8 property affected unless the Insured, before the occurrence of any loss or damage, obtains
9 the sanction of the Company signified by endorsement upon the policy by or on behalf of
10 the Company: -
11 a) If the trade or manufacture carried on be altered, or if the nature of the occupation of
12 or other circumstances affecting the building insured or containing the insured property
13 be changed in such a way as to increase the risk of loss or damage by Insured Perils.
14 b) If the building insured or containing the insured property becomes unoccupied and so
15 remains for a period of more than 30 days (Not applicable for Dwellings).
16 c) If the interest in the property passes from the insured otherwise than by will or
17 operation of law.
18
19 MARINE CLAUSE
20 4) This insurance does not cover any loss or damage to property which, at the time of the
21 happening of such loss or damage, is insured by or would, but for the existence of this
22 policy, be insured by any marine policy or policies except in respect of any excess beyond
23 the amount which would have been payable under the marine policy or policies had this
24 insurance not been effected.
25
26 CANCELLATION
27 5) This insurance may be terminated at any time at the request of the Insured, in which case
28 the Company will retain the premium at customary short period rate for the time the policy
29 has been in force.
30 This insurance may also at any time be terminated at the option of the Company, on 15
31 days' notice to that effect being given to the Insured, in which case the Company shall be
32 liable to repay on demand a rateable proportion of the premium for the unexpired term
33 from the date of the cancellation. It is however, further stated that such cancellation by the
34 insurer should only be exercised on ground of mis-representation, fraud, non-disclosure of
35 material facts and non-co-operation by the insured.
36
37 LOSS NOTIFICATION
38 6 (i) On the happening of any loss or damage the Insured shall forthwith give notice thereof to
39 the Company and shall within 15 days after the loss or damage, or such further time as the
40 Company may in writing allow in that behalf, deliver to the Company
41
42 a) A claim in writing for the loss or damage containing as particular an account as may
43 be reasonably practicable of all the several articles or items or property damaged or
4 | P a ge

1 destroyed, and of the amount of the loss or damage thereto respectively, having regard
2 to their value at the time of the loss or damage not including profit of any kind.
3
4 b) Particulars of all other insurances, if any
5

18 caused by centrifugal forces.

37
38 The powers conferred by this condition shall be exercisable by the Company at any time until
39 notice in writing is given by the insured that he makes no claim under the policy, or if any claim
40 is made, until such claim is finally determined or withdrawn, and the Company shall not by any
41 act done in the exercise or purported exercise of its powers hereunder, incur any liability to the
42 Insured or diminish its rights to rely upon any of the conditions of this policy in answer to any
43 claim.
44
5 | P a ge

1 If the insured or any person on his behalf shall not comply with the requirements of the
2 Company or shall hinder or obstruct the Company, in the exercise of its powers hereunder, all
3 benefits under this policy shall be forfeited.
4
5 The Insured shall not in any case be entitled to abandon any property to the Company whether
6 taken possession of by the Company or not.
7
8 FORFEITURE
9 8) If the claim be in any respect fraudulent, or if any false declaration be made or used in
10 support thereof or if any fraudulent means or devices are used by the Insured or any one
11 acting on his behalf to obtain any benefit under the policy or if the loss or damage be
12 occasioned by the willful act, or with the connivance of the Insured, all benefits under this
13 policy shall be forfeited.
14
15 OPTIONS TO REINSTATE
16 9) If the Company at its option, reinstate or replace the property damaged or destroyed, or
17 any part thereof, instead of paying the amount of the loss or damage, or join with any other
18 Company or Insurer(s) in so doing, the Company shall not be bound to reinstate exactly or
19 completely but only as circumstances permit and in reasonably sufficient manner, and in no
20 case shall the Company be bound to expend more in reinstatement than it would have cost
21 to reinstate such property as it was at the time of the occurrence of such loss or damage
22 nor more than the sum insured by the Company thereon.

If the Company so elect to
23 reinstate or replace any property the insured shall at his own expense furnish the Company
24 with such plans, specifications, measurements, quantities, and such other particulars as the
25 Company may require, and no acts done, or caused to be done, by the Company with a view
26 to reinstate or replace shall be deemed an election by the Company to reinstate or replace.
27
28 If in any case the Company shall be unable to reinstate or repair the property hereby
29 insured, because of any municipal or other regulations in force affecting the alignment of
30 streets or the construction of buildings or otherwise, the Company shall, in every such case,
31 only be liable to pay such sum as would be requisite to reinstate or repair such property if
32 the same could lawfully be reinstated to its former condition.
33
34 CONDITION OF AVERAGE
35 10) If the property hereby insured shall at the breaking out of any fire or at the commencement
36 of any destruction of or damage to the property by any other peril hereby insured against
37 be collectively of greater value than the sum insured thereon, then the Insured shall be
38 considered as being his own insurer for the difference and shall bear a rateable proportion
39 of the loss accordingly.

Every item, if more than one, of the policy shall be separately
40 subject to this condition.
41
42 CONTRIBUTION
43 11) If at the time of any loss or damage happening to any property hereby insured there be any
44 other subsisting insurance or insurances, whether effected by the Insured or by any other
6 | P a ge

1 person or persons covering the same property, this Company shall not be liable to pay or
2 contribute more than its rateable proportion of such loss or damage.
3
4 SUBROGATION
5 12) The Insured shall at the expense of the Company do and concur in doing, and permit to be
6 done, all such acts and things as may be necessary or reasonably required by the Company
7 for the purpose of enforcing any rights and remedies or of obtaining relief or indemnity
8 from other parties to which the Company shall be or would become entitled or subrogated,
9 upon its paying for or making good any loss or damage under this policy, whether such acts
10 and things shall be or become necessary or required before or after his indemnification by
11 the Company.
12
13 ARBITRATION
14 13) If any dispute or difference shall arise as to the quantum to be paid under this policy
15 (liability being otherwise admitted) such difference shall independently of all other
16 questions be referred to the decision of a sole arbitrator to be appointed in writing by the
17 parties to or if they cannot agree upon a single arbitrator within 30 days of any party
18 invoking arbitration, the same shall be referred to a panel of three arbitrators, comprising of
19 two arbitrators, one to be appointed by each of the parties to the dispute/difference and
20 the third arbitrator to be appointed by such two arbitrators and arbitration shall be
21 conducted under and in accordance with the provisions of the Arbitration and Conciliation
22 Act, 1996.
23
24 It is clearly agreed and understood that no difference or dispute shall be referable to arbitration
25 as hereinbefore provided, if the Company has disputed or not accepted liability under or in
26 respect of this policy.
27
28 It is hereby expressly stipulated and declared that it shall be a condition precedent to any right
29 of action or suit upon this policy that the award by such arbitrator/ arbitrators of the amount of
30 the loss or damage shall be first obtained.
31
32 NOTICE
33 14) Every notice and other communication to the Company required by these conditions must
34 be written or printed.
35
36 REINSTATEMENT OF SUM INSURED
37 15) At all times during the period of insurance of this policy the insurance cover will be
38 maintained to the full extent of the respective sum insured in consideration of which upon
39 the settlement of any loss under this policy, pro-rata premium for the unexpired period
40 from the date of such loss to the expiry of period of insurance for the amount of such loss
41 shall be payable by the insured to the Company.
42
43 The additional premium referred above shall be deducted from the net claim amount
44 payable under the policy.

This continuous cover to the full extent will be available
7 | P a ge

1 notwithstanding any previous loss for which the company may have paid hereunder and
2 irrespective of the fact whether the additional premium as mentioned above has been
3 actually paid or not following such loss.

The intention of this condition is to ensure
4 continuity of the cover to the insured subject only to the right of the company for deduction
5 from the claim amount, when settled, of pro-rata premium to be calculated from the date
6 of loss till expiry of the policy.
7
8 Notwithstanding what is stated above, the Sum Insured shall stand reduced by the amount of
9 loss in case the insured immediately on occurrence of the loss exercises his option not to
10 reinstate the sum insured as above.
11
12
13 E.

SPECIAL CONDITIONS:
14
15 a) Basis of Sum insured:
16
17 (i) Market Value Basis
18 It is a requirement of this Insurance that the sums insured stated in the Schedule, as declared
19 by the insured shall not be less than the  Market value  of the property insured.
20
21 (ii) Reinstatement Value Basis
22 The insured will also have an option to insure the property (except for stocks) on a
23  Reinstatement value basis , which shall be the cost of replacing or reinstating on the same site
24 or any other site with property of the same kind or type but not superior to or more extensive
25 then the insured property when new as on date of the loss, subject to special provisions
26 contained elsewhere in this Policy.
27
28 b) Basis of Settlement:
29
30 (i) Market Value settlement
31 It is the actual cost of the property lost or damaged at the time of loss (excluding profit of any
32 kind) taking into consideration the wear and tear and depreciation for usage.
33
34 (ii) Reinstatement Value settlement
35
36 The following are the provisions of Reinstatement Value Settlement
37
38 1.

The work of replacement or reinstatement (which may be carried out upon another site
39 and in any manner suitable to the requirements of the insured subject to the liability of
40 the Company not being thereby increased) must be commenced and carried out with
41 reasonable dispatch and in any case must be completed within 12 months after the
42 destruction or damage or within such further time as the Company may in writing allow,
43 otherwise no payment beyond the amount which would have been payable under the
44 policy if this memorandum had not been incorporated therein shall be made.
8 | P a ge

1
2 2.

Until expenditure has been incurred by the Insured in replacing or reinstating the
3 property destroyed or damaged the Company shall not be liable for any payment in
4 excess of the amount which would have been payable under the policy if this
5 memorandum had not been incorporated therein.
6
7 3.

If at the time of replacement or reinstatement the sum representing the cost which
8 would have been incurred in replacement or reinstatement if the whole of the property
9 covered had been destroyed, exceeds the Sum Insured thereon or at the
10 commencement of any destruction or damage to such property by any of the perils
11 insured against by the policy, then the insured shall be considered as being his own
12 insurer for the excess and shall bear a rateable proportion of the loss accordingly.

Each
13 item of the policy (if more than one) to which this memorandum applies shall be
14 separately subject to the foregoing provision.
15
16 4.

This Memorandum shall be without force or effect if
17
18 a) the Insured fails to intimate to the Company within 6 months from the date of
19 destruction or damage or such further time as the Company may in writing allow his
20 intention to replace or reinstate the property destroyed or damaged.
21
22 b) the Insured is unable or unwilling to replace or reinstate the property destroyed or
23 damaged on the same or another site.
24
25 Expenses Covered
26
27 (i) Architects, Surveyors and consulting engineer s fees (upto 3% of the claim amount)
28
29  It is hereby declared and understood that the expenses incurred towards Architects, Surveyors
30 and Consulting Engineers fees for plans, specification tenders, quantities and services in
31 connection with the superintendence of the reinstatement for the Building, Machinery,
32 Accessories and equipment insured under this policy is covered upto 3% of the adjusted loss,
33 but it is understood that this does not include any costs in connection with the preparation of
34 the Insured s claim or estimate of loss in the event of damage by insured perils .
35
36 (ii) Removal of debris clause (upto 1% of the claim amount)
37
38  It is hereby declared and agreed that the expenses incurred upto 1% of the claim amount is
39 included in the sum insured thereon for
40
41 (a) Removal of debris from the premises of the Insured;
42 (b) dismantling or demolishing;
43 (c) shoring up or propping. 
44
9 | P a ge

1 Note: (b) & (c) above should be deleted when neither Building nor Machinery are covered.
2
3 F.

DEFINITIONS:
4
5 1) Fire: Fire means combustion which is accidental accompanied by visible flame or glow
6 excluding destruction or damage caused to the property insured by (i) its own fermentation,
7 natural heating, or spontaneous combustion, (ii) its undergoing any heating or drying
8 process and (iii) burning of property insured by order of any Public Authority, -including
9 damage by fire initiated from such causes to the said property.

However, ensuing damage
10 by spread of fire to other property insured is payable provided such damage is not caused
11 by peril otherwise excluded under the policy.
12
13 2) Lightning: Loss or Damage Directly caused by Lightning
14
15 3) Explosion/Implosion: Damage excluding loss, destruction of or damage (a) to boilers (other
16 than domestic boilers), economizers or other vessels, machinery, or apparatus (in which
17 steam is generated) or their contents resulting from their own explosion/implosion, (b)
18 caused by centrifugal forces.
19
20 4) Aircraft Damage: Loss, Destruction or damage caused by Aircraft, other aerial or space
21 devices and articles dropped therefrom excluding those caused by pressure waves.
22
23 5) Riot, Strike, and Malicious Damage: Loss of or visible physical damage or destruction by
24 external violent means directly caused to the property insured but excluding those caused
25 by
26 i. total or partial cessation of work or the retardation or interruption or cessation of any
27 process or operations or omissions of any kind.
28 ii.

Permanent or temporary dispossession resulting from confiscation, commandeering,
29 requisition, or destruction by order of the Government or any lawfully constituted Authority.
30 iii.

Permanent or temporary dispossession of any building or plant or unit or machinery
31 resulting from the unlawful occupation by any person of such building or plant or unit or
32 machinery or prevention of access to the same.
33 iv.

Burglary, housebreaking, theft, looting, larceny or any such attempt or any omission of
34 any kind of any person (whether or not such act is committed in the course of a
35 disturbance of public peace) in any malicious act.
36
37 6) Storm, Cyclone, Typhoon, Tempest, Hurricane, Tornado, Flood, and Inundation: Loss,
38 destruction or damage directly caused by Storm, Cyclone, Typhoon, Tempest, Hurricane,
39 Tornado, Flood, or Inundation excluding those resulting from earthquake, Volcanic
40 eruption, or other convulsions of nature. (Wherever earthquake cover is given as an  add
41 on cover  the words  excluding those resulting from earthquake, volcanic eruption or other
42 convulsions of nature  shall stand deleted.)
43
44
10 | P a ge

1
2 7.

Bursting and/ or overflowing of water tanks, apparatus, and pipes:
3 Bursting or breaking of water tanks, pipes, or apparatus due to the buildup of internal
4 pressure beyond their capacity or breaking point and Overflowing is the escape of water
5 from the top of an overfilled container, water tanks, apparatus, or pipes
6
7 8.

Impact Damage:
8 Loss of or visible physical damage or destruction caused to the property insured due to
9 impact by any Rail/ Road vehicle or animal by direct contact not belonging to or owned
10 by (a) the Insured or any occupier of the premises or (b) their employees while acting in
11 the course of their employment.
12
13 9.

Subsidence and Landslide including Rock slide:
14 a) Loss, destruction, or damage directly caused by Subsidence of part of the site on
15 which the property stands or Land slide/Rock slide excluding:
16 b) the normal cracking, settlement or bedding down of new structures
17 c) the settlement or movement of made up ground
18 d) coastal or river erosion
19 e) defective design or workmanship or use of defective materials
20 f) demolition, construction, structural alterations or repair of any property or
21 groundworks or excavations.
22 10) Leakage from Automatic Sprinkler Installations: Excluding loss, destruction, or damage
23 caused by
24 a) Repairs or alterations to the buildings or premises
25 b) Repairs, Removal, or Extension of the Sprinkler Installation
26 c) Defects in construction known to the Insured.
27
28 11) Bush Fire: Loss excluding, destruction or damage caused by Forest Fire.
29
30 12) Terrorism:
31 An act of terrorism means an act or series of acts, including but not limited to the use of
32 force or violence and/or the threat thereof, of any person or group(s) of persons, whether
33 acting alone or on behalf of or in connection with any organisation(s) or government(s), or
34 unlawful associations, recognized under Unlawful Activities (Prevention) Amendment Act,
35 2008 or any other related and applicable national or state legislation formulated to combat
36 unlawful and terrorist activities in the nation for the time being in force, committed for
37 political, religious, ideological or similar purposes including the intention to influence any
38 government and/or to put the public or any section of the public in fear for such purposes.
39
40 13) Excess means the amount stated in the Schedule, which shall be borne by the Insured in
41 respect of each and every Claim made under this Policy.
42
11 | P a ge

1 14) Reinstatement Value: It is cost which would be incurred to replace the property insured at
2 the Premises with new items of similar kind and quality at any time during the Period of
3 Insurance.
4
5
6 15)  Market Value  means replacement value of insured property or item as New at the time of
7 Damage or Loss less due allowance for betterment, wear, and tear and/or depreciation on
8 the date of loss excluding the cost of land.
9
10 16)  Property Damage  means actual sudden and accidental physical damage to the Insured
11 Premises or property insured under the policy caused by the perils covered.
12
13
14 17)  Property  means assets, machinery, equipment, furniture, fixtures and fittings, electrical
15 installations and stock and stock-in-trade in the Insured s premises described in the
16 Schedule to this Policy including items contained therein for which the Insured is
17 accountable.
18
19 18)  Period of Insurance  means the period during which cover is provided by this Policy of
20 Insurance as specified in The Schedule.
21
22
23 19)  Proposal  means any signed proposal by filling up the questionnaires and declarations,
24 written statements and any information in addition thereto supplied to Company by Insured
25 or on Insured s behalf.
26
27 20)  Policy  means the Policy Booklet, the Schedule, the Proposal and any applicable
28 endorsements or memoranda.

The policy contains the details of the extent of the cover
29 available to the Insured, what is excluded from the cover and the conditions, warranties on
30 which the Policy is issued.
31
32
33 21)  Schedule  means the latest schedule issued by the Company as part of the Policy.

It
34 provides details of the Insured s Policy including full description of properties covered which
35 are in force and the period of cover against the properties described.
36
37 22)  Sum Insured  means the amount of insurance cover specified in The Schedule in respect of
38 any particular item of Property Insured or interest insured or the total amount of insurance
39 cover specified in The Schedule as the context may require.
12 | P a ge

1 23)  Occupancy  means the activity carried out in the insured premised stated in the schedule
2 and / or the purpose for which the same is used.
3
4 24)  Claim  means a claim under an Operative Clause in respect of an insured event.
5
13 | P a ge

full_text:
Standard Fire and Special Perils Policy (Commercial)
Policy Wordings
REGISTERED & CORP OFFICE:
Universal Sompo General Insurance Company Limited:
8th Floor & 9th Floor (South Side), Commerz International Business Park, Oberoi Garden City, Off
Western Express Highway, Goregaon East, Mumbai 400063.
Contact US:
24/7 Customer Care: Toll free Nos: 1800 - 22- 4030 or 1800-200-4030
Email: contactus@universalsompo.com
Website: www.universalsompo.com
IRDAI Registration Number:134
CIN: U66010MH2007PLC166770
Standard Fire and Special Perils Policy (Commercial) Page 1 of 16
UIN: IRDAN134CP0010V01201819

IN CONSIDERATION OF the Insured named in the Schedule hereto having paid to the Universal
Sompo General Insurance Company Limited (hereinafter called the Company) the full premium
mentioned in the said schedule, THE COMPANY AGREES, (Subject to the Conditions and Exclusions
contained herein or endorsed or otherwise expressed hereon) that if after payment of the premium the
Property insured described in the said Schedule or any part of such Property be destroyed or damaged
by any of the perils specified hereunder during the period of insurance named in the said schedule or
of any subsequent period in respect of which the Insured shall have paid and the Company shall have
accepted the premium required for the renewal of the policy, the Company shall pay to the Insured the
value of the Property at the time of the happening of its destruction or the amount of such damage or at
its option reinstate or replace such property or any part thereof
I. Fire
Excluding destruction or damage caused to the property insured by
a) i) Its own fermentation, natural heating or spontaneous combustion.
ii) It s undergoing any heating or drying process.
b) Burning of property insured by order of any Public Authority.
II. Lightning
III. Explosion/Implosion
Excluding loss, destruction of or damage
a) to boilers (other than domestic boilers), economizers or other vessels, machinery
or apparatus (in which steam is generated) or their contents resulting from their own
explosion/implosion.
b) caused by centrifugal forces.
IV. Aircraft Damage
Loss, Destruction or damage caused by Aircraft, other aerial or space devices and articles
dropped there from excluding those caused by pressure waves.
V. Riot, Strike and Malicious Damage
Loss of or visible physical damage or destruction by external violent means directly caused to
the property insured but excluding those caused by
a) total or partial cessation of work or the retardation or interruption or cessation of any
process or operations or omissions of any kind.
b) Permanent or temporary dispossession resulting from confiscation, commandeering,
requisition or destruction by order of the Government or any lawfully constituted
Authority.
c) Permanent or temporary dispossession of any building or plant or unit or machinery
resulting from the unlawful occupation by any person of such building or plant or unit or
Standard Fire and Special Perils Policy (Commercial) Page 2 of 16
UIN: IRDAN134CP0010V01201819

machinery or prevention of access to the same.
d) Burglary, housebreaking, theft, larceny or any such attempt or any omission of any kind of
any person (whether or not such act is committed in the course of a disturbance of public
peace) in any malicious act.
If the Company alleges that the loss/damage is not caused by any malicious act, the burden of
proving the contrary shall be upon the insured.
VI. Storm, Cyclone, Typhoon, Tempest, Hurricane, Tornado, Flood and Inundation
Loss, destruction or damage directly caused by Storm, Cyclone, Typhoon, Tempest, Hurricane,
Tornado, Flood or Inundation excluding those resulting from earthquake, Volcanic eruption or
other convulsions of nature. (Wherever earthquake cover is given as an  add on cover  the
words  excluding those resulting from earthquake volcanic eruption or other convulsions of
nature  shall stand deleted.)
VII. Impact Damage
Loss of or visible physical damage or destruction caused to the property insured due to impact
by any Rail/ Road vehicle or animal by direct contact not belonging to or owned by
a) the Insured or any occupier of the premises or
b) their employees while acting in the course of their employment.
VIII. Subsidence and Landslide including Rock slide
Loss, destruction or damage directly caused by Subsidence of part of the site on which the
property stands or Land slide/Rock slide excluding:
a) the normal cracking, settlement or bedding down of new structures
b) the settlement or movement of made up ground
c) coastal or river erosion
d) defective design or workmanship or use of defective materials
e) demolition, construction, structural alterations or repair of any property or ground works or
excavations.
IX. Bursting and/or overflowing of Water Tanks, Apparatus and Pipes
X. Missile Testing operations
XI. Leakage from Automatic Sprinkler Installations
Excluding loss, destruction or damage caused by
a) Repairs or alterations to the buildings or premises
b) Repairs, Removal or Extension of the Sprinkler Installation
c) Defects in construction known to the Insured.
XII. Bush Fire
Excluding loss, destruction or damage caused by Forest Fire.

or nuclear component thereof
